Cissus parva is a plant species within the grape family (Vitaceae) and is native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including arthritis, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cissus parva include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
